claims: The planning package is internally consistent and traceable: PRD P1-P6 map to ISS-1..4 and to named TDD tests.
Default route change to structured LiteLLM/Gemini is evidence-based and preserves cross-vendor independence (reviewer=Gemini, lead=Claude).
No gate-weakening, registry, fan-out, or executor wiring is introduced; non-goals are respected.
No implementation has been performed; this gate accepts the plan, not shipped code.
confidence_criteria: Phase 0 probe is reproducible and not cherry-picked
PRD promises map to issues and TDD tests with no orphan promises
Plan respects stated non-goals and gate semantics
All PRD and TDD grill findings are resolved
No hidden code changes claimed as done without diffs/tests
outcome.confidence_rationale: The planning artifacts are thorough, evidence-backed, and aligned to intent/scope/non-goals with all grill findings resolved, supporting acceptance of the plan. Confidence is held below 0.9 because the implementation and its safety-critical tests do not yet exist, and the central independence risk (supervisor wrapper validate-only vs repair) is unproven until execution.

assumptions_to_verify: evaluate_outcome_fidelity remains authoritative and is not bypassed for the structured path
Gateway/model failures classify as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable while invalid/truncated output classifies as reviewer_contract_unmet, never as accept
Read-only git-status guard wraps the new reviewer route
Existing proceed_degraded/escalate recovery tests still pass after the change
contradictions_checked: Command labels gate 'outcome_review' but workflow current_gate is 'implementation_plan' and only planning artifacts exist -> resolved as a planning-package review; flagged that no code is present
Claim of cross-vendor independence vs default reviewer lineage -> Gemini via LiteLLM is non-Claude, consistent with P3/G2
Claim that structured JSON preserves the typed contract vs risk of contract loosening -> plan mandates wrap-then-evaluate_outcome_fidelity (G3), unverified in code
missing_evidence: Implementation code for reviewer_model/reviewer_output_mode in config.py and the LiteLLM path in cursor_agent.py
Passing runs of the TDD-named tests (structured success, contract-miss, infra-failure, revise/deny blocking, read-only guard, route metadata)
Proof that the wrapper validates-only and does not repair verdicts
critical_review.strongest_objection: The structured route requires the supervisor to construct the <dual_agent_outcome> wrapper; if implementation repairs or synthesizes missing fields instead of validating only, it would manufacture a fake independent verdict and defeat the cross-vendor independence this slice exists to restore.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: At the execution gate, failing or absent tests proving the wrapper repairs/synthesizes verdicts, bypasses evaluate_outcome_fidelity, misclassifies a valid revise/deny as recoverable infrastructure, or drops the read-only guard would flip this to revise/deny.

objections: Acceptance is scoped to the implementation-plan/planning package only; no implementation code or TDD-named tests exist in the working tree yet (config.py lacks reviewer_model/reviewer_output_mode; cursor_agent.py lacks the LiteLLM path), so this must not be read as implementation-complete.

Execution must prove with tests that the supervisor's <dual_agent_outcome> wrapping only validates schema JSON and never repairs or synthesizes missing fields, otherwise cross-vendor independence is silently defeated.

specialists.name: Claude Lead Reviewer
specialists.objection: No implementation code or tests exist yet; acceptance is scoped to plan soundness, and execution must prove the structured-route wrapper validates-only (never repairs verdicts)

outcome.summary: Reviewed the reviewer-contract-reliability planning package (PRD P1-P6, issues ISS-1..4, TDD, implementation-plan, Phase 0 probe). Workflow current_gate is implementation_plan with prd/tdd/issues accepted; only planning artifacts exist in the tree and no implementation code/tests are present yet, which is correct for this stage. Phase 0 evidence (Cursor SDK 0/8 errored-empty, LiteLLM Gemini 3.1 Pro strict-schema 3/3) decisively justifies defaulting the independent reviewer to the structured LiteLLM/Gemini route while keeping Cursor SDK as explicit compatibility mode. The plan preserves the typed Outcome contract via wrap-then-evaluate_outcome_fidelity, keeps revise/deny in the AND blocking algebra, keeps the read-only guard and reviewer_unavailable_policy recovery, and respects all non-goals. All ten grill findings (G1-G5, TG1-TG5) are resolved. Accept the plan; execution must land ISS-1..4 code plus the named RED tests and prove the supervisor wrapper validates-only and never repairs/synthesizes verdicts.
